title: 破镜谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\137-破镜谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Following the sudden illness of Heather Badecock at the gathering, Mrs. Bantry informs a man that she does not know a woman living in the local residential development, though she notes the woman's husband is an inconspicuous middle-aged man. Nearby, Ella Jelinsky enters a bathroom to search for ammonia solution, a stimulant. Mrs. Bantry subsequently encounters Jason Radley, who informs her that the woman is dead.

The following morning, Miss Knight serves breakfast to Miss Marple. Miss Knight mentions that Mrs. Bantry had called at eight o'clock to speak with Miss Marple, but the message was delayed until after breakfast. While Miss Marple eats, Miss Knight complains about a young woman named Shirley singing loudly while vacuuming.

Thelma (also referred to as Sheila or Shelley) visits Miss Marple to discuss the incident at Gossington Hall. She reports that Mrs. Badecock died during a fundraising event for St. John's Ambulance after drinking something and feeling unwell. Although Mrs. Badecock had been in perfect health, a doctor ordered an autopsy because the cause of death was undetermined. Thelma describes Mrs. Badecock as a kind but meddlesome person who often shared herb-filled savory cakes. Thelma notes that while Mrs. Badecock's husband appeared devastated, he may have simply lacked the courage to stand up to his wife.

After Miss Knight informs Miss Marple that Mrs. Bantry has gone out, Mrs. Bantry arrives at Miss Marple's home, out of breath and stating she has much to tell her.
